business.type_label: solo podcasting educator — online course + e-book + newsletter (Marek Jankowski)

engine.ladder_reasoning: The purchase decision hinges on evaluating a productized THING — a 6-module / 71-lesson course and a 130-page e-book — via curriculum outline, sample lessons, bonuses, tiered pricing and outcome proof. The personal brand and founder origin story function as proof, not as the primary hire-a-person decision that trust implies.

goal.one_action: Homepage front door captures name+email opt-in to unlock a free training video (lead magnet); deeper pages sell the course (3-tier) and e-book via checkout.
goal.delegated_to: convertbox/tally (opt-in); course+ebook checkout with external login at wiedza.malawielkafirma.pl

structure.archetype_note: Info-product funnel: lead-magnet homepage feeding a paid course (3-tier checkout) + one-time e-book sale, plus a content blog and an alumni-outcomes showcase used as social proof. Multi-job property, not one conversion action.

mapped.observed_as: Korzyści z własnego podcastu (6 benefit cards)
Zobacz nagranie... (email+name opt-in to unlock video)
Myślisz o własnym podcaście? / Najtrudniejsze masz już za sobą
Ja też tego nie umiałem / About the author (23 yrs mic)
Co wchodzi w skład kursu? (6 modules, 71 lessons) / Są też bonusy!
Pakiety i ceny (Silver 1097 / Gold 1197 / Platinum 2397 PLN)
Często zadawane pytania / Ile mam czasu na przerobienie kursu?
Absolwenci kursu (200+ graduate podcasts, 12 topic filters)
Start reading today / Dołącz do kursu

unmapped.observed: Baza wiedzy — category-organized browsable article grid (Podstawy, Nagrywanie i sprzęt, Promocja i zarabianie, Słuchanie)
unmapped.why_no_fit: A multi-category browsable knowledge feed is content-property scope, not a single landing-page section.

proposed_new.name: course-curriculum
proposed_new.evidence: Paid course with a 6-module/71-lesson outline (Co wchodzi w skład kursu?), detailed Agenda kursu, sample-lesson previews, bonuses, certificate, tiered enrollment, PLUS the Absolwenci page (200+ graduate podcasts, 12 topic filters) — matches the registered canonical exactly, including its alumni/graduate-showcase clause.

proposed_new.name: storefront-checkout
proposed_new.evidence: On-site one-time-goods checkout: course tiers with package-selection + payment-method + invoice fields (BLIK/card/transfer/installments) and e-book 'Kup teraz / Zamów za 149 zł'.

proposed_new.name: content-index
proposed_new.evidence: Baza wiedzy is a category-organized browsable grid of articles (4 categories), a feed/index rather than a single blog-teaser strip.

design_style.none_fits_because: Conversion-optimized info-product funnel (VWO, Meta Pixel, Google Ads, Clarity, benefit-card grids, long persuasion, tiered pricing). design_hints empty (no fonts/colors captured) so inferred from structure; runner-up warm/human given the solo-educator personal voice.

confidence.why: fetch_quality=full so section/CTA/commerce data is rich, but the ICP call is genuinely borderline (clean lead-magnet homepage vs a real 3-tier course checkout + e-book purchase pushes it out of single-conversion ICP), engine thing-vs-trust is debatable, and design_hints are empty so design_style is inferred.

gap_findings: Mixed-job property: one domain runs a lead-magnet front door AND a course checkout AND a one-time e-book sale AND a blog AND an alumni directory — the taxonomy assumes ONE conversion action per site; nothing expresses a multi-job funnel where the homepage is in-ICP but deeper pages transact.

Lead-magnet gated-content opt-in (email unlocks a training video) is functionally distinct from a plain contact lead-form and from newsletter subscribe, yet collapses to 'form' in the mechanism taxonomy.
Alumni/student-outcome directory as a proof form is only representable by folding it into course-curriculum; there is no canonical section id for a browsable outcomes/results directory.
